creator: shane@itlab.co.nz
text:
I have a script that generates a list of pending build targets and a list of targets this machine can build, and generates the intersection list using grep -Fxf. On our Mac it was behaving strangely and I've tracked and reduced it to the following snippets.

Although found on OSX, I've reproduced this bug using the bsdgrep command in FreeBSD 9.0-RELEASE (bsdgrep -V -> "bsdgrep (BSD grep) 2.5.1-FreeBSD"). The grep on OSX is bsdgrep, while on my BSD it is GNU grep.


# -F=fixed strings, -x=match whole lines, -f=read patterns from file
printf "linux
bsd
macosx64
" | bsdgrep -Fxf <(printf "macosx
macosx64
")
gives no result, but the following work fine.

# swap pattern order
printf "linux
bsd
macosx64
" | bsdgrep -Fxf <(printf "macosx64
macosx
")
# search for prefix substring
printf "linux
bsd
macosx
" | bsdgrep -Fxf <(printf "macosx
macosx64
")
# in either pattern order
printf "linux
bsd
macosx
" | bsdgrep -Fxf <(printf "macosx64
macosx
")

It seems to fail to match a line that contains an earlier pattern match as a prefix.

I will simply avoid this problem by using comm instead, but I'll hang around for a while to answer any questions that come up.
